name: Voltage Drop Calculator
seo_title: Voltage Drop Calculator – % and Volts – IEC 60364
seo_description: Calculate voltage drop in electrical cables by cross-section, length and current. Free tool checks if your wiring meets the 3-5% limit. Instant results.

range_hints.seccion_mm2: Installed conductor section: 1.5, 2.5, 4, 6, 10, 16, 25, 35, 50, 70, 95 or 120 mm² per IEC 60228
range_hints.longitud_m: One-way cable length from supply to load. For single-phase circuits consider both outbound and return path
range_hints.corriente_a: Steady-state load current. Do not confuse with short-circuit current

result_context: Cable of {seccion_mm2}mm² a {length_m}m with {corriente_a}A tiene caída of {caida_v}V ({caida_pct}%). ¿Cumple? {cumple}. Caída máxima permitida: {caida_max_v}V.
formula_display: Caída_V = (2 × length × current) ÷ (cross-section × conductividad); Caída_% = (caída_V ÷ voltage) × 100
mistakes: Aceptar caída > 3% in circuitos of iluminación
Not Consider que cables agrupados disipan less heat
Confusing conductividad of the cobre (56) with aluminio (35)
trust_note: Calculation based on annealed electrolytic copper conductivity at 20 °C (σ = 56 m/(Ω·mm²), 100 % IACS). For aluminum use σ = 35 m/(Ω·mm²). At elevated operating temperatures the actual σ value decreases, increasing voltage drop.
standard: IEC 60364-5-52: 3 % for lighting circuits, 5 % for power circuits. NEC Article 210.19(A) limits combined feeder and branch circuit drop to 5 % in North America. Measurement from installation origin to the farthest utilization point.
